mnemonics.title: DPDA Determinism Rule
mnemonics.content: ONE MOVE MAX: For any state-symbol-stack combo, at most ONE transition exists. Think: 'One choice, no guess work, deterministic success!'

mnemonics.title: DCFL Properties
mnemonics.content: COMP N-I-R: Closed under Complement, Intersection with Regular, NOT closed under Union/Star/Concatenation. Remember: 'Complement yes, Union no'.

mnemonics.title: DPDA vs NPDA Power
mnemonics.content: DPDA < NPDA < Turing Machine. NPDA can guess (non-determinism), DPDA cannot. Remember: 'D for Deficient compared to N'.

mnemonics.title: Stack Operations
mnemonics.content: PUSH adds top, POP removes top, REPLACE replaces top. LIFO: Last In, First Out - think of a stack of plates.

acronyms.usage: Finite automaton with stack, deterministic transitions, recognizes DCFL
Language recognized by DPDA, proper subset of CFL, closed under complement
Grammar generating DCFL, exactly one parse tree per string
Language recognized by any PDA (including NPDA)
Automaton with stack memory, accepts CFL
Stack operation principle - top element accessed first

visualTips.visualization: Draw stack as a vertical container with bottom Z0. Push adds to top (left in notation), Pop removes from top. Think of stack of books: add/remove from top only.
Show (q1, aab, XYZ) ⊢ (q2, ab, YZ) as: state changes, input head moves right one symbol, top of stack X replaced with YZ.
Circles for states, arcs labeled 'a, X/γ' meaning 'read a, pop X, push γ'. Use double circle for final states, Z0 at bottom of stack initially.
DPDA: one arrow for each input-stack combo. NPDA: branching arrows (like guessing). Draw as single path vs tree of possibilities.

analogies.analogy: Stack is like a stack of cafeteria trays - you can only add or remove from the top. Can't reach plates in middle without removing top ones first.
Deterministic is like following a precise recipe with one instruction per step. Non-deterministic is like having multiple recipes for same dish and trying all simultaneously.
DCFL is like arithmetic expressions with unambiguous grammar (only one parse). CFL is like natural language with multiple interpretations possible.
DPDA parsing is like a calculator following fixed按键顺序 - predictable, efficient. NPDA is like trying all possible interpretations of ambiguous sentence.
